humanities_role_explanation: Designed specifically for evaluating graduate-level historical knowledge and global history comprehension using structured historical data.
benchmark_modalities_explanation: A text-only benchmark presenting multiple-choice historical questions formulated from the structured Seshat Global History Databank.
analytical_tasks_explanation: Evaluates factual recall and understanding of historical societies spanning different periods (Neolithic to Industrial Revolution) and geographical regions.

general_purpose_benchmark.note: Specifically designed to evaluate deep, specialized historical knowledge rather than a general suite of capabilities.

evaluates_generative_llm.note: Evaluates seven generative models from the OpenAI, Gemini, and Llama families, such as Llama-3.1-8B and GPT-4-Turbo.

llm_is_primary_system.note: Generative LLMs are the primary systems under evaluation to assess their historical accuracy.

arts_humanities_data.note: Focuses on the ASJC area of History.

quantitative_metrics.note: Evaluated using balanced accuracy on a 4-choice format.

published_after_march_2023.note: Published as part of the NeurIPS 2024 Datasets and Benchmarks track.

english_language.note: The paper and the underlying benchmark are in English.

summary: The paper introduces HiST-LLM, a specialized text benchmark based on the Seshat Global History Databank to test LLMs on expert-level, global history knowledge across 600 societies from the Neolithic to the Industrial Revolution. It evaluates seven state-of-the-art LLMs, identifying substantial performance disparities depending on the historical epoch and geographical region.
key_findings: The evaluated LLMs achieve a balanced accuracy ranging from 33.6% (Llama-3.1-8B) to 46% (GPT-4-Turbo) in a 4-choice setting, outperforming random guess (25%) but remaining below expert comprehension. Performance is stronger for earlier historical periods, with notable regional imbalances (lowest accuracy in Oceania and Sub-Saharan Africa for advanced models).
